heading: (Optional) Regionalized Private Endpoints for Multi-Region Sharded Clusters
body:
For global sharded clusters that you deploy in multiple regions, if you need
to connect to Atlas using a private endpoint from networks that can't
be peered with one another, you can deploy multiple private
endpoints to a region.You can deploy any number of private endpoints to regions that you
deployed your cluster to. Each regional private
endpoint connects to the mongos instances in that region.WARNING: Your [connection strings](connstring-privatelink) to existing
multi-region and global sharded clusters change when you enable this
setting.You must update your applications to use the new connection strings.
This might cause downtime.You can enable this setting if your Atlas project
contains only sharded clusters.You can't disable this setting if you have:* More than one private endpoint in more than one region, or

* More than one private endpoint in one region and one private
  endpoint in one or more regions.You can only create sharded clusters when you enable the regionalized
private endpoint setting. You can't create replica sets.To use this feature, you must enable the regionalized private
endpoint setting.To enable or disable the regionalized private endpoint setting:**Atlascli:**Atlas CLI
